content:
Hello, I recently got a drawing tablet and trying to write a device driver for it. I know I need to "interact" with the device over USB, and I have most of that code understood. My problem is not knowing where to begin to set up my "environment" for linux device driver development in Rust.

Some questions on my mind are:
How do I include linux kernel headers?
What do I target my builds for?
How do I run it?
There aren't any abstractions for USB in-tree yet, but @Martin Rodriguez Reboredo has patchset for it https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/884
Qemu supports USB emulation https://qemu-project.gitlab.io/qemu/system/devices/usb.html you should be able to do a standard rust-enabled kernel build and run it in qemu
(@Antonio Hickey in case you have notifications off)

There's a pair of things to consider when developing USB drivers, one is that generally you don't write an USB driver directly, you instead write drivers for other subsystems that are on top of it, like hid, input or usb-serial
If I'd to submit my USB bindings it will only be bindings for USB interfaces and devices, drivers are kinda pointless due to the previous point
Also the USB driver interface is not very much useful by itself, but other subsystems make good use of it

Hello @Martin Rodriguez Reboredo , I'm not sure I understand.

So I shouldn't be looking to use USB directly for building my device driver, but instead things built on top of it? I wanted to do this in Rust, so would using hid, input, or usb-serial be possible to work with right now?

It mainly depends on what device you want to write drivers for. Those three subsystem don't have yet any bindings, so if you want to target them you'll need their bindings, alongside the USB ones too. By the way, do you have any specific USB device in mind?

I'm pretty sure I need to use hid along side the USB ones to get the functionality I need.

The USB device is the XP-Pen Artist 22 drawing tablet. I'm currently using their Proprietary driver, but want to use something open source and also get a chance to explore lower level development.

Would those 3 subsystems be something we will want to eventually create bindings for? I'd be interested in trying to help contribute to this and USB stuff too.
XP-Pen Artist 22 would likely fell under hid-uclogic or hid-wacom, drivers based on hid. But you can write a driver apart from both of them for your tablet, we'd need hid bindings for it.
And to usb if some functionality from it is required.
